title: Arduino 3D Scanner — Stepper Turntable + TOF Sensor
design_question: How to build a low-cost 3D scanner using Arduino, a rotating turntable and a distance sensor?
selected_approach: NEMA17 stepper motor (1.8°/step) rotates object turntable (200 steps = 1 revolution, 1.8° resolution). VL53L1X TOF sensor (I2C, 4 m range, 1 mm accuracy) measures radial distance at each step. Arduino Uno controls stepper via DRV8825, reads TOF, transmits r,θ pairs via Serial to PC. Python processing script converts to Cartesian (x=r·cos(θ), y=r·sin(θ)), plots point cloud, exports to CSV/PLY. Multiple vertical scan heights via manual z-axis adjustment.
rationale: VL53L1X provides 1 mm accuracy sufficient for objects > 50 mm. NEMA17 + DRV8825 gives smooth 1/16 microstepping for fine angular resolution. Arduino Uno handles sensor polling and stepper control simultaneously. Python processing is open-source and extensible.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires precise lighting and many photos.

verification_notes: FACT: '3D Scanning — Structured Light and Laser Triangulation' confirms x=r·cos(θ), y=r·sin(θ) conversion and scan time estimation.
supporting_fact_titles: 3D Scanning — Structured Light and Laser Triangulation
Stepper Motor Drive — Full Step and Microstepping
